channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Authorities investigate North Hills family deaths as suspected murder-suicide involving mother
item.description: Los Angeles Police Department homicide detectives are investigating the deaths of four family members, including two adults and two young children, in a North Hills home in the San Fernando Valley as ...

item.title: Como The Treasury Perth: a decade on, WA luxury hotel praised for calm design and service
item.description: Como The Treasury in Perth is presented as one of Australia’s most refined luxury hotels, celebrated a decade after opening for its calm, considered design and exceptional service. Housed within Perth...

item.title: Adversaries are exploiting commercial phone location data to track US troops after years of Pentagon warnings
item.description: For nearly a decade, US defense officials, contractors, and intelligence analysts repeatedly warned the Pentagon that commercially available smartphone location data could expose the movements of Amer...

item.title: Questions raised over $200,000 government communications contract awarded without tender in antisemitism envoy work
item.description: Antisemitism envoy Jillian Segal has come under scrutiny after selecting a consulting firm linked to a former senior adviser to former prime minister Scott Morrison for a $200,000 government contract ...

item.title: US Allocates KSh 10.3 Billion for Ebola Response in East Africa as Kenya Agrees to Host Quarantine Facility for Americans
item.description: The United States government has released KSh 10.3 billion as emergency funding to help fight the Ebola outbreak affecting parts of East Africa, mainly the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda. Thi...

item.title: Airport Bag-Switching Scandal Highlights Gaps in Canadian Aviation Security
item.description: A recent investigation by CTV has revealed a troubling pattern of organised crime exploiting weak security measures at Toronto’s Pearson Airport. Airport employees allegedly swapped passengers' luggag...

item.title: Civil society protests in Pretoria urge Agriculture Minister to address food insecurity in South Africa
item.description: More than 120 people from civil society organisations gathered outside the Department of Agriculture in Pretoria under the banner of the Union Against Hunger (UAH), calling on Agriculture Minister Joh...

item.title: Twelve injured in two separate village clashes in Nuh following Eid prayers linked to old disputes
item.description: Clashes broke out in two villages of Nuh district in Haryana shortly after Bakrid (Eid al-Adha) prayers, resulting in at least 12 people being injured across two separate incidents. According to polic...

item.title: US proposal may require immigrants to apply for green cards from their home country, affecting families
item.description: The United States government recently issued a draft policy memorandum which could change the way green card applications are handled. Traditionally, legal immigrants in the US could apply for a green...

item.title: Kahama road works to be completed on schedule under TACTIC Project
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The government, through Prof Riziki Shemdoe, Minister of State in the Prime Minister’s Office (Regional Administration and Local Government), has made it clear that there will be no ex...

item.title: Government to construct multiple dams along SGR corridor to manage floods and climate risks
item.description: The Tanzanian government is planning to build more than nine dams along the Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) corridor, aiming to control repeated floods and improve climate resilience in affected regions....

item.title: Austrian court convicts man over planned attack on Taylor Swift Vienna concert
item.description: An Austrian court has convicted a 21-year-old man for his involvement in a plot to attack a Taylor Swift concert in Vienna, a plan that led to the cancellation of the singer’s shows in Austria in Augu...

item.title: Pampanga building collapse in Angeles City leaves five dead as search and rescue operations continue
item.description: Rescue operations in Angeles City, Pampanga continue after a building collapsed in Barangay Balibago before dawn on Sunday, resulting in multiple casualties and people still feared trapped under debri...

item.title: Elite runners gear up for 11th Okpekpe International 10km Road Race in Edo State
item.description: As Edo State dey ginger up for the 11th edition of the Okpekpe International 10km Road Race, top runners from around the world don start dey arrive. Among them na Ethiopian rising star Lemi Teshome we...

item.title: Government funds youth-led cage fish farming projects in Lake Victoria to boost production and jobs
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has released more than Sh1.3 billion to support over 40 youths in starting cage fish farming projects in Lake Victoria, specifically in Musoma Municipality, Mara Region. Thi...

item.title: SARB increases repo rate to 7% as inflation pressures from oil and food costs mount
item.description: The South African Reserve Bank (SARB) has raised its main policy interest rate by 25 basis points to 7%, citing increasing inflation risks driven largely by global oil price volatility and rising food...

item.title: New Brunswick man released from prison after serving sentence for 2010 kidnapping and sexual assault
item.description: A man from New Brunswick has been released from prison after serving a sentence for kidnapping and sexually assaulting a woman in 2010. The case, which drew significant public attention, has left the ...

item.title: Gracie Abrams announces major 2026–2027 arena tour across North America and Europe
item.description: Gracie Abrams has announced an extensive arena tour titled ‘The Look At My Life Tour’, set to take place across North America, the United Kingdom and mainland Europe over 2026 and 2027. The tour follo...

item.title: Robin Padilla faces legal complaint over Bato dela Rosa’s escape; Estrada charged in flood control case
item.description: Philippine Senator Robin Padilla is facing an obstruction of justice complaint after the Philippine National Police confirmed that Bato dela Rosa did not merely hitch a ride in Padilla's vehicle durin...

item.title: Former GOP strategist discusses Democratic social media response to Stephen Miller and political tensions online
item.description: The article reports on commentary from former Republican strategist Rick Wilson regarding a recent online exchange involving White House Deputy Chief of Staff Stephen Miller and the official Democrati...
